steps.title: Find the hypotenuse
steps.content:
The problem gives $AB=16$ and says side $AC$ is $25$ greater than side $AB$:

$$AC=16+25=41$$

Since angle $B$ is $90^\circ$, side $AC$ is the hypotenuse.

steps.title: Use the altitude relation
steps.content:
In a right triangle, the altitude from the right angle to the hypotenuse gives the area in two equivalent ways:

$$\frac{1}{2}AB\cdot BC=\frac{1}{2}AC\cdot BD$$

Cancel $\frac{1}{2}$ and divide by $BD\cdot AB$:

$$\frac{BC}{BD}=\frac{AC}{AB}$$
